title: Deciphering the single-cell molecular landscape of ampullary cancer: A rare gastrointestinal malignancy.
snippet: Ampullary carcinoma (AC) is a rare epithelial cancer, representing around 0.2% of all gastrointestinal malignancies worldwide, with an estimated incidence of 0.73 per 100,000 people. The rarity of AC, combined with the intricate anatomy of its site of origin, often complicates histopathological diagnosis. Recent advances in molecular research, however, have...

title: Extracellular vesicles as biomarkers of disease progression and therapeutic response in patients with spinal muscular atrophy.
snippet: Spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) is a devastating genetic disorder characterized by loss of motor neurons and muscle atrophy. In the most severe form, affected infants experience progressive weakness and, if untreated, typically do not survive beyond 2 years of age. Although several disease-modifying therapies are currently available, treatment response varies...

ASCO’s biggest winners showcased a new era of cancer innovation
snippet: Revolution Medicines stole the show at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting as full data from its pancreatic cancer drug lived up to expectations, while Summit and Akeso proved the PD-(L)1/VEGF mechanism and Eli Lilly showed that its in vivo CAR T bet is paying off.

title: NewLimit snags $435M after seeing age reversal in human liver cells
snippet: NewLimit is pressing the gas, speeding into clinical trials much sooner than expected after lab research showed its epigenetic reprogramming asset reversed aging in human liver cells.

title: Celcuity slides even after ‘practice-changing’ late-stage breast cancer data
snippet: Celcuity’s gedatolisib doubled progression-free survival versus standard of care in certain patients with advanced breast cancer. Still, the biotech’s stock dropped more than 25% Tuesday.

title: Travere beefs up rare kidney disease portfolio with up to $1.1B China deal
snippet: Travere Therapeutics will gain an exclusive license to the oral BTK inhibitor civorebrutinib, which analysts at Guggenheim Securities said could be “complementary” to the biotech’s IgA nephropathy drug Filspari.

title: FDA taps prior knowledge to expedite cell and gene therapy development
snippet: A year of significant policy change at the FDA brought momentum and scrutiny into the new year. As 2026 gets underway, biopharma companies are responding to sweeping vaccine changes while concerns surface about the politicization of the agency.

title: Legend soars on ‘best-in-class,’ ‘best-in-disease’ in vivo CAR T in early lymphoma study
snippet: All six non-Hodgkin lymphoma patients on Legend Biotech’s CAR T therapy responded to treatment—findings that could make the biotech an attractive takeover target, according to analysts at Oppenheimer.

title: Lilly targets kidney disease with Ascidian’s RNA exon editors for up to $1.9B
snippet: For an investment of up to $1.9 billion, Eli Lilly will be able to use Ascidian Therapeutics’ platform that removes mutated exons from mRNA molecules, avoiding the expression of disease-causing proteins.

title: ASCO wins from RevMed, Akeso/Summit, more; plus more Lilly and more China; ADA on deck
snippet: Improved survival on display at the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) in Chicago; Pfizer’s unusual pact with China’s Innovent highlights a new type of collaboration; Eli Lilly continues its nonstop deal streak, including with Chinese biotechs; and looking ahead to this weekend’s American Diabetes Associatio...

title: Facing pressure from China, BioSpace’s NextGen biotech leaders say: ‘Bring it on’
snippet: From Chinese innovation to AI, biotech CEOs are being hit with challenges at a breakneck pace. Three leaders from BioSpace’s NextGen Class of 2026 told us about the issues keeping them up at night.

title: No longer a bargain pool, Chinese biotechs command higher premiums
snippet: Deal dynamics between Chinese biotechs and global pharma companies are changing fast, with the biotechs seeking higher upfront payments and the Big Pharmas seeking more expansive platform deals.

title: Pharma manufacturing projected to create 19,000 jobs through 2034
snippet: The forecast makes pharma one of the fastest-growing manufacturing industries in the U.S.

title: Anthropic Funding Pushed Startup Investment To Near-Record Levels In May As Exit Market Reopened

title: Epigenetic and genetic insights for tailored therapeutic strategies in multiple sclerosis.
snippet: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is a complex neurological disorder with inflammation, demyelination, and neurodegeneration in the CNS. Disease-modifying therapies (DMTs) exist, but their efficacy is limited by MS's varied nature, highlighting the need for personalized treatments. title: Integrating genomic features for prognosis in Chinese patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma following R-CHOP therapy.
snippet: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) exhibits 30-40% rates of relapse or refractory disease after R-CHOP. Identifying patients at risk for treatment failure is essential for planning subsequent therapeutic strategies, including stem cell mobilization or cellular therapy. title: Review Article: Systemic Treatments for Advanced Cholangiocarcinoma-State of the Art and Future Directions.
snippet: Cholangiocarcinoma (CCA) is a heterogeneous group of biliary tract malignancies with increasing global incidence and poor prognosis. CCA is anatomically classified into intrahepatic (iCCA), perihilar (pCCA) and distal (dCCA) subtypes with distinct biological and clinical features. title: Managing cytokine release syndrome in hematological malignancies being treated with CAR-T therapy.
snippet: Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) is a potentially life-threatening toxicity associated with chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-T cell therapy, particularly in hematological malignancies. As CAR-T therapies are increasingly integrated into clinical practice, managing CRS effectively is critical to maximizing therapeutic benefit while minimizing harm. This review...

title: Merck continues New Jersey cuts, laying off 88 in Rahway
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: ASCO: Pfizer scores in lung, colorectal and prostate cancer but key readouts still to come
snippet: Pfizer showcased multiple late-breakers at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual conference but its biggest data are expected later this year.

title: ASCO: Merck overshadowed by upstart biotechs but TROP2 plans begin to pay off
snippet: Merck may not have had the splashiest presentation at the American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting, but the data show why the Big Pharma remains king of oncology, analysts say.

title: Fulcrum tanks as cancer concerns stymie sickle cell asset, trigger business reorg
snippet: Fulcrum Therapeutics’ is down more than 50% Tuesday morning as the biotech discontinues development of its lead asset. Ipsen’s Tazverik, which belongs to the same class, was pulled from the market in March due to the risk of secondary malignancies.

title: Lilly bets $3B+ to add China’s Haisco to growing list of partners in five-program pact
snippet: Eli Lilly and the Haisco Pharmaceutical Group have yet to disclose what specific indications they plan to prioritize.

title: ASCO: Moderna’s mRNA-based melanoma vaccine shows ‘encouraging’ 5-year survival
snippet: When used alongside Merck’s Keytruda, Moderna’s personalized cancer vaccine halved the risk of death or disease recurrence in a mid-stage trial of advanced melanoma—a result analysts said could help “instill confidence” in the regimen.

title: Big Pharma makes ‘creative effort’ to fold Chinese assets into pipelines
snippet: Recent deals from Pfizer and Bristol Myers Squibb hint at Big Pharma’s more collaborative approach to partnering with Chinese companies.

title: France’s biotech ambition is strong but its scale-up engine still needs work
snippet: Government backing, deep scientific talent and a robust pharma heritage are helping France punch above its weight, but turning research into investable companies remains a challenge.

title: BrainStorm levels up, bringing on former FDA leader Peter Pitts for new ALS bid
snippet: For Peter Pitts, a former associate commissioner at the FDA, the appointment to the board of BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics is an opportunity to fulfill a promise he made long ago to a patient with ALS.

title: Single-cell RNA sequencing unveils macrophage heterogeneity and cell-cell interactions in hepatocellular carcinoma progression.
snippet: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) represents a major global health burden, characterized by complex metabolic reprogramming and immunological dysregulation. This study aimed to elucidate the molecular mechanisms underlying HCC progression using integrative multi-omics analyses, with a specific focus on macrophage heterogeneity and intercellular communication n...

title: Senescence-associated secretory phenotype: the "pathogenic" factor driving orthopedic degenerative diseases and its regulation.
snippet: Orthopedic degenerative diseases, including osteoarthritis (OA), intervertebral disc degeneration (IVDD), and osteoporosis (OP), are major causes of chronic pain and functional decline in aging populations worldwide. The senescence-associated secretory phenotype (SASP), a downstream but central effector of cellular senescence, has emerged as a key pathogeni...

title: Multi-Omics and Single-Cell Dissection Reveals EXT1 as a Glycosylation-Linked Therapeutic Target in Cancer.
snippet: Glycosylation and inflammation are pivotal in tumor progression, yet the specific glycosyltransferases bridging these processes remain poorly defined. This study investigated Exostosin-1 (EXT1), a key enzyme in heparan sulfate (HS) biosynthesis, as a mechanistic bridge connecting inflammation, stromal remodeling, and immune evasion-driven cancers.We used a....

title: Cell Segmentation as Strategic Decision Making.
snippet: Cell segmentation in imaging-based spatial transcriptomics (ST) relies on stained image segmentation or transcript clustering, but current methods are limited by dependence on manual annotations, sensitivity to staining quality and cell density, and lack of alignment with true transcriptomic profiles. title: Characterization of the tumor microenvironment in locally advanced gastric cancer and identification of spatially predictive biomarkers associated with beneficial neoadjuvant immunochemotherapy.
snippet: Neoadjuvant immunochemotherapy (nICT) has emerged as a promising strategy for locally advanced gastric cancer (LAGC), yet clinical responses remain heterogeneous and reliable predictive biomarkers are lacking. title: A scissor-guided single-cell framework defines a macrophage-derived risk score for prognostic and immunotherapy stratification in lung adenocarcinoma.
snippet: Macrophages are key regulators within the lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) tumor microenvironment, and their functional states and interaction patterns are closely linked to prognosis and immunotherapy response.
